summary: Crisp soundboard recording of an energetic 1986 show that delivers the first-ever Hand Jive with the Neville Brothers and several standout jams like Cassidy, HJK and Johnny B. Goode.
review: The tape captures a high‑energy Mardi Gras‑era performance highlighted by the historic Hand Jive, a soaring Cassidy jam and a ripping Johnny B. Goode solo from Jerry.  While most reviewers rave about the clear soundboard mix and overall excitement, a few note a weak Scarlet→Fire pairing and some unevenness in the Estimated transition.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars and praise both the performance and sound quality; only a few minor criticisms keep the average just below an “exceptional” rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp and clear with excellent balance; no major drop‑outs or muffling reported, only occasional minor mix quirks noted by a single reviewer.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Jack Straw (HJK)
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is a roller‑coaster – strong opening with Sugaree and Bucket, a wildly enthusiastic middle (HJK, Scarlet→Fire, Estimated), followed by an explosive second half featuring the Neville Brothers’ Drums Part II, Hand Jive and a high‑octane encore.  The flow feels uneven to some listeners but overall remains highly engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crystal‑clear tone and leading jams (Hand Jive, Johnny B. Goode), though a few notes mention him slipping early in the Estimated jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for stepping up on Cassidy and keeping solid bass foundation throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for energetic vocals and stage presence (“spitting” on audience) and strong contributions during jams like HJK.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards shine during Drums Part II with the Neville Brothers, adding extra drive to the rhythm section.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey & Bill (Billy) sometimes described as a bit off‑kilter early, but the extended drums segment with the Nevilles is praised for being louder and faster.
